name: Kalki
shortDescription: Kalki is the tenth and final avatar of Vishnu, prophesied to appear at the end of Kali Yuga to restore dharma and usher in a new Satya Yuga.
description: Kalki is the final incarnation of Vishnu, the preserver god in the Hindu Trimurti. He is prophesied to appear at the end of Kali Yuga, the current epoch characterized by moral and societal decay. Kalki is depicted as a warrior riding a white horse named Devadatta, wielding a blazing sword. His arrival signifies the destruction of evil, the re-establishment of dharma (righteousness), and the beginning of a new Satya Yuga, or Golden Age. The Kalki Purana details Kalki's birth, his training, his battles against adharma, and his eventual triumph. He is often associated with themes of apocalypse, renewal, and messianic hope. While some interpretations view Kalki as a literal future event, others see him as a symbolic representation of the cyclical nature of time and the constant struggle between good and evil. His role is crucial in the cosmic cycle of creation, preservation, and destruction, mirroring archetypal figures in other mythologies who herald the end of an era and the dawn of a new one. The concept of Kalki shares similarities with messianic figures and prophecies of world renewal found in various cultures.
